toolUseResult.originalFile:
# agent-sync

[![Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync?label=release)](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/releases/latest)

`agent-sync` is a Go CLI that keeps AI-agent configuration for multiple tools
(Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Pi, Antigravity — with more planned) in sync
from a single Git-backed manifest. Think of it as `dotfiles` for agents: one
canonical source of skills, rules, commands, and MCP servers; native files
rendered into each tool's own conventions.

> **Status:** pre-alpha. v1 is in active development. Architecture and
> rationale live in the [requirements](docs/brainstorms/2026-04-21-aienvs-agent-workspace-requirements.md)
> and [implementation plan](docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md).

## Install

Download the prebuilt binary for your platform, verify its checksum, and put it
on your PATH — one command:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/main/install.sh | sh
```

Installs to `/usr/local/bin` (falling back to `~/.local/bin` if that isn't
writable). Pin a version or change the location with env vars:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/main/install.sh \
  | AGENT_SYNC_VERSION=v0.1.0 AGENT_SYNC_INSTALL_DIR="$HOME/bin" sh
```

Prefer not to pipe to a shell? Download the archive for your OS/arch from the
[Releases page](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/releases), verify it
against `checksums.txt`, extract the `agent-sync` binary, and move it onto your
PATH. (Windows: grab the `.zip`.) Git must be on your PATH at runtime.

## Quickstart

New to agent-sync? [`docs/quickstart.md`](docs/quickstart.md) walks through
authoring a canonical repo and running your first `init → sync → validate`,
with a copyable example under [`examples/canonical/`](examples/canonical/).
The short version: `agent-sync init` needs zero flags — the source defaults
to the in-repo `.agents` directory and targets are discovered from the tool
footprints (`.claude/`, `.cursor/`, ...) already in your workspace.

## What agent-sync does

1. **Manifests form a hierarchy.** A `.agent-sync.yaml` binds a directory to a
   canonical source and declares which target tools receive configuration. The
   source is one of: a remote Git repo (`url`) or a local clone (`local_path`),
   both pinned by commit SHA; or an in-repo working-tree directory (`local_dir`,
   e.g. `.agents`) for per-repo skills authored right in the repo — unpinned,
   and exempt from trust and offline-strict since there's nothing remote to fetch.
   `sync` walks up from the current directory (project root = nearest `.git`
   ancestor) and emits every manifest it finds, each to its own scope; the
   user-level manifest at `~` is emitted only with `sync --user`. agent-sync
   never merges across levels — each target tool resolves precedence via its own
   native config hierarchy, and `sync` warns when a scope emits a kind a tool
   won't read natively at that level.
2. **Explicit `sync`.** `agent-sync sync` materializes the pinned content,
   compiles it via per-tool adapters, stages the output, and atomically
   swaps it into each tool's reserved subdirectory (e.g.
   `.claude/rules/agent-sync/`, `.cursor/rules/agent-sync/`, `.codex/skills/agent-sync-<id>/`,
   `.agent/rules/agent-sync/` for Antigravity). An interactive sync offers to
   include the user-level (`~`) manifest in the same run; non-interactive runs
   note when the user scope was skipped so it never drifts silently.
   Emitted skills carry a real `name` / `description` frontmatter block so
   they show up correctly in each tool's skill list, and every managed file
   records its source (`<url>@<short-sha>`) in its header.
3. **Moving the pin: `update`.** `agent-sync update` fetches the canonical
   remote, shows what changed since the current pin, and — on confirmation
   (or `--accept-update=<sha>` when non-interactive) — re-pins
   `commit` + `trusted_sha` and re-syncs in one locked step. It is
   fast-forward-only: a rewritten upstream history is refused unless you
   pass `--accept-rewritten-history=<sha>`. Use `--user` to update the
   `~` manifest.
4. **Safe by default.** Pinning, offline-strict, TOFU on `(URL, SHA)`
   pairs, reserved-prefix ownership with a ledger, and atomic swap with
   rollback — designed to fail closed.
5. **Capability-honest translation.** A tool-agnostic IR (`agents-md`,
   `rule`, `skill`, `command`, `plugin-reference`, `mcp-server-entry`) is
   translated by each adapter; per-target capability reports make
   lossy translations visible.

## Supported tools

Adapters translate the tool-agnostic IR into each tool's native files. Five
adapters ship bundled today; the rest are planned, and the `Planned (tier)`
label records the support tier each will land at.

| Tool | Status |
|------|--------|
| [Claude Code](docs/adapters/claude.md) | ✅ Bundled |
| [Cursor](docs/adapters/cursor.md) | ✅ Bundled (agents-md, rule, skill, command, mcp) |
| [Codex CLI](docs/adapters/codex.md) | ✅ Bundled |
| [Pi](docs/adapters/pi.md) (`@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`) | ✅ Bundled (agents-md, skill, command) |
| [Antigravity](docs/adapters/antigravity.md) (2.0 IDE + CLI; replaces Gemini CLI, retired 2026-06-18) | ✅ Bundled (full parity) |
| Windsurf | Planned (experimental) |
| LM Studio | Planned (experimental) |

## Requirements

- Go 1.25+
- Git (shelled out for network operations)

## Build

```bash
go build ./cmd/agent-sync
```

## Test

```bash
go test -race ./...
golangci-lint run
```

## Project layout

See [`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md`](docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md)
for the complete repo layout. The short version:

```
cmd/agent-sync/    CLI entry point
internal/          All implementation packages; no external API yet
docs/              Requirements, plans, specs
```

## Contributing

See [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for guidance when
working on `agent-sync` itself (including from inside a coding agent). These
files are **in-repo agent guidance for contributors**, not output of the
sync pipeline.

## License

Licensed under the [Apache License 2.0](LICENSE). See [`NOTICE`](NOTICE) for
attribution requirements.

**Quality process applied:** every batch was diff-reviewed and independently `-race`-verified, not trusted on Codex's self-report. Two adversarial review passes ran — one before the activation (caught the caller-contingent revoke defense → I added a structural fail-closed guard) and one after (caught a TOCTOU ff-baseline, an empty-`trusted_sha` divergence, and a routing-predicate coupling → all three fixed and tested). The security rails are verified: revoke always wins, ff-only, `--frozen`/`auto:false` opt-outs, offline-falls-back, non-interactive never prompts.

Once the gate confirms green, the branch is ready for a PR (`ce-commit-push-pr`). I won't push or open it without your go-ahead — want me to once it's confirmed?

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/justinbeaudry/.claude/plugins/cache/compound-engineering-plugin/compound-engineering/3.9.3/skills/ce-commit-push-pr

# Git Commit, Push, and PR

**Asking the user:** When this skill says "ask the user", use the platform's blocking question tool: `AskUserQuestion` in Claude Code (call `ToolSearch` with `select:AskUserQuestion` first if its schema isn't loaded), `request_user_input` in Codex, `ask_user` in Gemini, `ask_user` in Pi (requires the `pi-ask-user` extension). Fall back to presenting the question in chat only when no blocking tool exists in the harness or the call errors (e.g., Codex edit modes) — not because a schema load is required. Never silently skip the question.

## Mode

- **Description-only** — user wants *just* a description ("write/draft a PR description", "describe this PR", or pasted a PR URL/number alone). Run Step 4 only; print the result. Apply only if the user asks. If a PR ref was pasted, pass it to Step 4 so Pre-A resolves the right range.
- **Description update** — user wants to refresh/rewrite an existing PR's description with no commit/push intent. If no open PR, report and stop. Otherwise run Step 4 (PR mode using the existing PR's URL), then Step 5 to preview, confirm, and apply via `gh pr edit`.
- **Full workflow** — otherwise. Run Steps 1-5 in order.

## Context

**On platforms other than Claude Code**, run the Context fallback below. **In Claude Code**, the labeled sections contain pre-populated data — use them directly.

**Git status:**
On branch feat/inline-auto-update
nothing to commit, working tree clean

**Working tree diff:**
(Bash completed with no output)

**Current branch:**
feat/inline-auto-update

**Recent commits:**
36491a1 docs: revise threat model T1, CHANGELOG, README for auto-advance (U8)
8f1af84 feat(sync): wire per-scope auto-advance + old->new audit (U5, U7)
30fa697 fix(trust): fail closed when allow-new-shas posture lacks loaded state
53b5412 feat(trust): shared advance core + allow-new-shas auto posture
d63b96e feat(sync): canonical.auto schema, resolve-or-fallback materialize, --frozen flag
eb81444 docs: brainstorm + plan for inline auto-update
eb62168 docs: add agent harness hierarchy design
e53317b feat: zero-flag init (source default + target discovery) and user-scope sync ergonomics (#42)
2bcd843 docs: stamp CHANGELOG 0.7.0
d4f0bab feat(engine): file-leaf OutputMode; Cursor & Pi commands (#41)

**Remote default branch:**
origin/main

**Existing PR check:**
NO_OPEN_PR

### Context fallback

```bash
printf '=== STATUS ===\n'; git status; printf '\n=== DIFF ===\n'; git diff HEAD; printf '\n=== BRANCH ===\n'; git branch --show-current; printf '\n=== LOG ===\n'; git log --oneline -10; printf '\n=== DEFAULT_BRANCH ===\n'; git rev-parse --abbrev-ref origin/HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo 'DEFAULT_BRANCH_UNRESOLVED'; printf '\n=== PR_CHECK ===\n'; gh pr view --json url,title,state 2>/dev/null || echo 'NO_OPEN_PR'
```

---

## Step 1: Resolve branch and PR state

The remote default branch returns something like `origin/main`; strip the `origin/` prefix. If it returned `DEFAULT_BRANCH_UNRESOLVED` or bare `HEAD`, try `gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef --jq '.defaultBranchRef.name'`. If both fail, fall back to `main`.

Branch routing:

- **Detached HEAD** — explain a branch is required and ask whether to create a feature branch. If yes, derive a name from the change content. If no, stop.
- **On default branch with work to do** (uncommitted, unpushed, or no upstream) — automatically create a feature branch (pushing the default directly is not supported). Derive a name from the change content and continue at Step 3, which handles branch creation safely. Do not ask whether to branch — committing on the default is not an option here.
- **On default branch with no work** — report no feature branch work and stop.
- **Feature branch** — continue.

Note the existing PR URL from the PR check if `state: OPEN`. Step 5 uses it to route between new-PR and existing-PR application.

## Step 2: Determine conventions

Match repo style for commit messages and PR titles (project instructions in context > recent commits > conventional commits as default). With conventional commits, default to `fix:` over `feat:` when ambiguous — adding code to remedy broken or missing behavior is `fix:`. Reserve `feat:` for capabilities the user could not previously accomplish. The user may override.

## Step 3: Commit and push

If on the default branch, branch creation needs to handle stale local `<base>`, unpushed commits on local `<base>`, and uncommitted changes that collide with the fresh remote base. Read `references/branch-creation.md` and follow its decision flow before continuing.

Scan changed files for naturally distinct concerns. If they clearly group into separate logical changes, create separate commits (2-3 max). Group at file level only — no `git add -p`. When ambiguous, one commit is fine.

Stage and commit each group. **Avoid `git add -A` and `git add .`** — they sweep in `.env`, build artifacts, and generated files:

```bash
git add file1 file2 file3 && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
commit message here
EOF
)"
```

Then push:

```bash
git push -u origin HEAD
```

If the working tree is clean and all commits are already pushed, this step is a no-op.

## Step 4: Compose the PR title and body

**You MUST read `references/pr-description-writing.md`** in full — the core principle at the top governs every step. The only input it needs from this skill is the PR ref, if one was identified by mode dispatch (description-only with a pasted URL, or description update).

**Evidence decision** before composition. Two short-circuits, then the full decision:

1. **User explicitly asked for evidence** ("ship with a demo", "include a screenshot") — proceed directly to capture. If capture is impossible or clearly not useful, note briefly and proceed without.
2. **Agent judgment on authored changes** — if you authored the commits and know the change is non-observable (internal plumbing, type-only, backend refactor without user-facing effect, docs/markdown/changelog/CI/test-only, pure refactors), skip the prompt without asking.

Otherwise, if the branch diff changes observable behavior (UI, CLI output, API behavior with runnable code, generated artifacts, workflow output) and evidence is not blocked (unavailable credentials, paid services, deploy-only infrastructure, hardware), ask: "This PR has observable behavior. Capture evidence for the PR description?"

- **Capture now** — load `ce-demo-reel` with a target description from the branch diff. It returns `Tier`, `Description`, `URL`, `Path`. Exactly one of `URL`/`Path` contains a real value; the other is `"none"`. If `URL`, splice as a `## Demo` section. If `Path` (user chose local save), note in the body that a demo was recorded but is not embedded. If skipped, proceed without evidence.
- **Use existing evidence** — ask for the URL or markdown embed; splice as a `## Demo` section.
- **Skip** — proceed without an evidence section.

Then continue with the rest of the reference (Steps A through G) to compose the title and body.

## Step 5: Apply and report

**Description-only mode** — print the title and body. Stop unless the user asks to apply.

**New PR** (full workflow, no existing PR from Step 1) — apply per "Applying via gh" below using `gh pr create`. Report the URL.

**Existing PR** (full workflow, found in Step 1) — the new commits are already on the PR from Step 3. Report the PR URL, then ask whether to rewrite the description.

- **No** — done.
- **Yes** — run Step 4 if not already done, then preview and apply (see below).

**Description update mode, or existing-PR rewrite confirmed** — preview before applying. Ask: "New title: `<title>` (`<N>` chars). Summary leads with: `<first two sentences>`. Total body: `<L>` lines. Apply?" If declined, the user may pass focus text back for a regenerate; do not apply. If confirmed, apply per "Applying via gh" below using `gh pr edit` and report the URL.

---

## Applying via gh

The body **must** be written to a temp file and passed via `--body-file <path>`. Never use `--body-file -`, stdin pipes, heredoc-to-stdin, or `--body "$(cat ...)"` — wrappers and stdin handling can silently produce an empty PR body while `gh` still exits 0 and returns a URL.

```bash
BODY_FILE=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/ce-pr-body.XXXXXX") && cat > "$BODY_FILE" <<'__CE_PR_BODY_END__'
<the composed body markdown goes here, verbatim>
__CE_PR_BODY_END__
```

The quoted sentinel keeps `$VAR`, backticks, and any literal `EOF` inside the body from being expanded.

For `<TITLE>`: substitute verbatim. If it contains `"`, `` ` ``, `$`, or `\`, escape them or switch to single quotes.

```bash
gh pr create --title "<TITLE>" --body-file "$BODY_FILE"   # new PR
gh pr edit   --title "<TITLE>" --body-file "$BODY_FILE"   # existing PR
```

1	# PR Description Writing
2	
3	## The core principle
4	
5	The diff is already visible on GitHub. The description exists to explain what the diff cannot show: what was impossible before and is now possible, what was broken and is now fixed, what shape changed. Cut any sentence a reader could reconstruct from the diff itself.
6	
7	- Bad: "Adds `evidence-decider.ts`, modifies `ce-commit-push-pr/SKILL.md` to call it, and updates two test files."
8	- Good: "Evidence capture now decides automatically whether a change has observable behavior. CLI tools and libraries are now eligible alongside web UIs."
9	
10	If the lead sentence describes what was moved, renamed, or added rather than what's now possible or fixed, rewrite it. This applies to every section, not just the opening — restating the diff is the failure mode this skill exists to prevent.
11	
12	For user-facing bugs, run an extra before/after pass before writing the mechanism: name what the user would have seen before and what they now see instead. Only then mention the technical cause or fix, and only if it helps the reviewer understand risk. A lead like "Playback hooks now ignore late async responses" is still too mechanical if the visible bug was "old videos, thumbnails, or errors could appear after switching selections."
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Step Pre-A: Resolve the range and base
17	
18	Two modes:
19	
20	- **Current-branch mode** (default) — describe HEAD vs the repo's default base.
21	- **PR mode** — describe a specific PR. Triggered when the caller passes a PR ref.
22	
23	For PR mode, fetch metadata first:
24	
25	```bash
26	gh pr view <ref> --json baseRefName,headRefOid,url,body,state,isCrossRepository,headRepositoryOwner
27	```
28	
29	If `state` is not `OPEN`, report and stop — do not invent a description. Use `baseRefName` as `<base>` and `headRefOid` as `<head>`.
30	
31	For current-branch mode, resolve `<base>` in priority order: caller-supplied (`base:<ref>`) → `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref origin/HEAD` (strip `origin/`) → `gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef --jq '.defaultBranchRef.name'` → try `main`/`master`/`develop` via `git rev-parse --verify origin/<candidate>`. If none resolve, ask the user. `<head>` is `HEAD`.
32	
33	**Base remote:** `origin` for current-branch mode and same-repo PRs. For fork PRs, match the PR's base owner/repo against `git remote -v`. If no local remote matches, skip to the `gh` fallback — do not diff against `origin` (wrong base).
34	
35	```bash
36	git fetch --no-tags <base-remote> <base>
37	git fetch --no-tags <base-remote> <head>   # PR mode only: <head> is headRefOid and may not be local
38	git log  --oneline "<base-remote>/<base>..<head>"
39	git diff           "<base-remote>/<base>...<head>"
40	```
41	
42	If the commit list is empty, report "No commits to describe" and stop.
43	
44	**Fallback** — use `gh pr diff <ref>` and `gh pr view <ref> --json commits` when local git can't reach the refs (fork PR with no matching remote, shallow clone, offline, merge-base on unrelated histories). For GHES configurations that reject SHA fetch but allow `refs/pull/`:
45	
46	```bash
47	git fetch --no-tags <base-remote> "refs/pull/<number>/head"
48	PR_HEAD_SHA=$(awk '/refs\/pull\/[0-9]+\/head/ {print $1; exit}' "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)/FETCH_HEAD")
49	```
50	
51	Note in the user-facing summary when the API fallback was used.
52	
53	---
54	
55	## Step A: Size the description
56	
57	Match weight to weight. When in doubt, shorter wins. Subtract fix-up commits (review fixes, lint, rebase resolutions) when sizing — they're invisible to the reader. Large PRs need more selectivity, not more content.
58	
59	| Change profile | Description approach |
60	|---|---|
61	| Small + simple (typo, config, dep bump) | 1-2 sentences, no headers. Under ~300 characters. |
62	| Small + non-trivial (bugfix, behavioral change) | 3-5 sentences. No headers unless two distinct concerns. |
63	| Medium feature or refactor | Narrative frame, then what changed and why. Call out design decisions. |
64	| Large or architecturally significant | Narrative frame + 3-5 design-decision callouts + brief test summary. Target ~100 lines, cap ~150. For PRs with many mechanisms, use a Summary table; do not create an H3 per mechanism. |
65	| Performance improvement | Include before/after measurements as a markdown table. |
66	
67	For small + simple PRs, the value-led sentence is the entire description.
68	For small + non-trivial bugfixes, the 3-5 sentence target still needs a user-visible before/after lead when the bug affected UI, CLI output, workflow output, or any other user-observable behavior. Concision is not a reason to skip the visible symptom.
69	
70	---
71	
72	## Step B: Compose the title
73	
74	`type: description` or `type(scope): description`.
75	
76	- Type by intent, not file extension. When `fix` and `feat` both seem to fit, default to `fix` — adding code to remedy missing behavior is `fix`. Reserve `feat` for capabilities the user could not previously accomplish. Use `refactor`/`docs`/`chore`/`perf`/`test` when more precise.
77	- Scope (optional): narrowest useful label. Omit when no single label adds clarity.
78	- Description: imperative, lowercase, under 72 chars, no trailing period.
79	- Match repo conventions visible in recent commits.
80	- **Never use `!` or `BREAKING CHANGE:` without explicit user confirmation** — they trigger automated major-version bumps.
81	
82	---
83	
84	## Step C: Assemble the body
85	
86	In order: opening → body sections that earn their keep → test plan if non-obvious → evidence block if one exists → Compound Engineering badge after a `---` rule.
87	
88	The opening goes under `## Summary` if the body uses any `##` headings; bare paragraph otherwise. No orphaned opening paragraphs above the first heading.
89	
90	**Evidence handling:** preserve any existing `## Demo` or `## Screenshots` block verbatim unless the user's focus asks to refresh it. If the caller passed a freshly captured URL or path, splice as `## Demo`. Otherwise omit. Place before the badge. Never label test output as "Demo" or "Screenshots."
91	
92	**Visual aids:** reach for a diagram or table when it conveys the change faster than prose — relationships, flows, state transitions, sequences, trade-offs, before/after data, or any structure prose would have to enumerate. Mermaid and markdown tables cover most shapes; don't be limited to a particular type if a different one fits the change better. Place inline at the point of relevance. Skip for simple, prose-clear, or rename/dep-bump changes. Prose is authoritative when it conflicts with a visual.
93	
94	**GitHub gotchas:** never prefix list items with `#` (GitHub auto-links `#1` as an issue ref). Use `org/repo#123` or full URL for actual references.
95	
96	---
97	
98	## Step D: Badge
99	
100	```markdown
101	---
102	
103	[![Compound Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_with-Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin)
104	![HARNESS](https://img.shields.io/badge/MODEL_SLUG-COLOR?logo=LOGO&logoColor=white)
105	```
106	
107	| Harness | `LOGO` | `COLOR` |
108	|---|---|---|
109	| Claude Code | `claude` | `D97757` |
110	| Codex | (omit `?logo=` param) | `000000` |
111	| Gemini CLI | `googlegemini` | `4285F4` |
112	
113	**Model slug:** spaces become underscores; append context window and thinking level in parens if known. **URL-encode literal parens as `%28` / `%29`** — unencoded parens inside markdown image URLs break release-please's commit parser, which silently drops the commit from the changelog. Examples: `Opus_4.6_%281M,_Extended_Thinking%29`, `Sonnet_4.6_%28200K%29`, `Gemini_3.1_Pro`.
114

# PR Description Writing

## The core principle

The diff is already visible on GitHub. The description exists to explain what the diff cannot show: what was impossible before and is now possible, what was broken and is now fixed, what shape changed. Cut any sentence a reader could reconstruct from the diff itself.

- Bad: "Adds `evidence-decider.ts`, modifies `ce-commit-push-pr/SKILL.md` to call it, and updates two test files."
- Good: "Evidence capture now decides automatically whether a change has observable behavior. CLI tools and libraries are now eligible alongside web UIs."

If the lead sentence describes what was moved, renamed, or added rather than what's now possible or fixed, rewrite it. This applies to every section, not just the opening — restating the diff is the failure mode this skill exists to prevent.

For user-facing bugs, run an extra before/after pass before writing the mechanism: name what the user would have seen before and what they now see instead. Only then mention the technical cause or fix, and only if it helps the reviewer understand risk. A lead like "Playback hooks now ignore late async responses" is still too mechanical if the visible bug was "old videos, thumbnails, or errors could appear after switching selections."

---

## Step Pre-A: Resolve the range and base

Two modes:

- **Current-branch mode** (default) — describe HEAD vs the repo's default base.
- **PR mode** — describe a specific PR. Triggered when the caller passes a PR ref.

For PR mode, fetch metadata first:

```bash
gh pr view <ref> --json baseRefName,headRefOid,url,body,state,isCrossRepository,headRepositoryOwner
```

If `state` is not `OPEN`, report and stop — do not invent a description. Use `baseRefName` as `<base>` and `headRefOid` as `<head>`.

For current-branch mode, resolve `<base>` in priority order: caller-supplied (`base:<ref>`) → `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref origin/HEAD` (strip `origin/`) → `gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef --jq '.defaultBranchRef.name'` → try `main`/`master`/`develop` via `git rev-parse --verify origin/<candidate>`. If none resolve, ask the user. `<head>` is `HEAD`.

**Base remote:** `origin` for current-branch mode and same-repo PRs. For fork PRs, match the PR's base owner/repo against `git remote -v`. If no local remote matches, skip to the `gh` fallback — do not diff against `origin` (wrong base).

```bash
git fetch --no-tags <base-remote> <base>
git fetch --no-tags <base-remote> <head>   # PR mode only: <head> is headRefOid and may not be local
git log  --oneline "<base-remote>/<base>..<head>"
git diff           "<base-remote>/<base>...<head>"
```

If the commit list is empty, report "No commits to describe" and stop.

**Fallback** — use `gh pr diff <ref>` and `gh pr view <ref> --json commits` when local git can't reach the refs (fork PR with no matching remote, shallow clone, offline, merge-base on unrelated histories). For GHES configurations that reject SHA fetch but allow `refs/pull/`:

```bash
git fetch --no-tags <base-remote> "refs/pull/<number>/head"
PR_HEAD_SHA=$(awk '/refs\/pull\/[0-9]+\/head/ {print $1; exit}' "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)/FETCH_HEAD")
```

Note in the user-facing summary when the API fallback was used.

---

## Step A: Size the description

Match weight to weight. When in doubt, shorter wins. Subtract fix-up commits (review fixes, lint, rebase resolutions) when sizing — they're invisible to the reader. Large PRs need more selectivity, not more content.

| Change profile | Description approach |
|---|---|
| Small + simple (typo, config, dep bump) | 1-2 sentences, no headers. Under ~300 characters. |
| Small + non-trivial (bugfix, behavioral change) | 3-5 sentences. No headers unless two distinct concerns. |
| Medium feature or refactor | Narrative frame, then what changed and why. Call out design decisions. |
| Large or architecturally significant | Narrative frame + 3-5 design-decision callouts + brief test summary. Target ~100 lines, cap ~150. For PRs with many mechanisms, use a Summary table; do not create an H3 per mechanism. |
| Performance improvement | Include before/after measurements as a markdown table. |

For small + simple PRs, the value-led sentence is the entire description.
For small + non-trivial bugfixes, the 3-5 sentence target still needs a user-visible before/after lead when the bug affected UI, CLI output, workflow output, or any other user-observable behavior. Concision is not a reason to skip the visible symptom.

---

## Step B: Compose the title

`type: description` or `type(scope): description`.

- Type by intent, not file extension. When `fix` and `feat` both seem to fit, default to `fix` — adding code to remedy missing behavior is `fix`. Reserve `feat` for capabilities the user could not previously accomplish. Use `refactor`/`docs`/`chore`/`perf`/`test` when more precise.
- Scope (optional): narrowest useful label. Omit when no single label adds clarity.
- Description: imperative, lowercase, under 72 chars, no trailing period.
- Match repo conventions visible in recent commits.
- **Never use `!` or `BREAKING CHANGE:` without explicit user confirmation** — they trigger automated major-version bumps.

---

## Step C: Assemble the body

In order: opening → body sections that earn their keep → test plan if non-obvious → evidence block if one exists → Compound Engineering badge after a `---` rule.

The opening goes under `## Summary` if the body uses any `##` headings; bare paragraph otherwise. No orphaned opening paragraphs above the first heading.

**Evidence handling:** preserve any existing `## Demo` or `## Screenshots` block verbatim unless the user's focus asks to refresh it. If the caller passed a freshly captured URL or path, splice as `## Demo`. Otherwise omit. Place before the badge. Never label test output as "Demo" or "Screenshots."

**Visual aids:** reach for a diagram or table when it conveys the change faster than prose — relationships, flows, state transitions, sequences, trade-offs, before/after data, or any structure prose would have to enumerate. Mermaid and markdown tables cover most shapes; don't be limited to a particular type if a different one fits the change better. Place inline at the point of relevance. Skip for simple, prose-clear, or rename/dep-bump changes. Prose is authoritative when it conflicts with a visual.

**GitHub gotchas:** never prefix list items with `#` (GitHub auto-links `#1` as an issue ref). Use `org/repo#123` or full URL for actual references.

---

## Step D: Badge

```markdown
---

[![Compound Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_with-Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin)
![HARNESS](https://img.shields.io/badge/MODEL_SLUG-COLOR?logo=LOGO&logoColor=white)
```

| Harness | `LOGO` | `COLOR` |
|---|---|---|
| Claude Code | `claude` | `D97757` |
| Codex | (omit `?logo=` param) | `000000` |
| Gemini CLI | `googlegemini` | `4285F4` |

**Model slug:** spaces become underscores; append context window and thinking level in parens if known. **URL-encode literal parens as `%28` / `%29`** — unencoded parens inside markdown image URLs break release-please's commit parser, which silently drops the commit from the changelog. Examples: `Opus_4.6_%281M,_Extended_Thinking%29`, `Sonnet_4.6_%28200K%29`, `Gemini_3.1_Pro`.

Skip the badge if regenerating a body that already contains it.

content.text:
Branch pushed. This is a large, security-significant change, so I'll write a narrative PR body with a mechanism table, the safety-rail design, the breaking/migration note, and the review summary. Composing and opening now.

One note before I do: I'm **not** putting `!`/`BREAKING CHANGE:` in the title (the skill withholds those pending explicit confirmation since they trigger automated major bumps). It's documented loudly in the body and CHANGELOG instead — tell me if you want the `!` marker added for release tooling.

input.command:
cd /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync
BODY_FILE=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/ce-pr-body.XXXXXX") && cat > "$BODY_FILE" <<'__CE_PR_BODY_END__'
## Summary

`agent-sync sync` now keeps a workspace current with its canonical source on its own: it advances the pinned commit to the newest upstream commit by default, instead of only ever materializing the commit a human previously pinned. Tracking upstream no longer requires the separate, manually-gated `agent-sync update`.

> [!WARNING]
> **This is a breaking, semver-major change.** Existing pinned workspaces begin tracking upstream on their next `sync`. Opt out per run with `--frozen` or per workspace with `canonical.auto: false`. CI and reproducibility-sensitive pipelines should use an opt-out. See migration below.

Because this removes human review from the default path for moving the pin, the advance is fenced by safety rails rather than trust alone — advancement is fast-forward-only, a revoked anchor always refuses, and the whole thing routes *through* the existing trust policy engine, not around it.

## What changed

| Area | Change |
|---|---|
| `canonical.auto` (manifest) | New `*bool` opt-out; `nil` ⇒ auto-on, `false` ⇒ pinned-only. |
| `sync --frozen` (flag) | Per-run opt-out; restores pinned + gated behavior. |
| Materialize | New resolve-or-fallback mode: resolve newest online, fall back to the cached pin on network failure. |
| Advance core | `resolve → fast-forward check → re-pin` extracted from `update` into a shared helper both paths use. |
| Trust policy | New `allow-new-shas` posture + `KindProceedAutoAdvance`; auto-advances append `pending.jsonl` for audit. |
| Sync wiring | Per-scope decision at prepare time; run-lock held across re-pin + emit; hierarchy stays continue-and-report. |
| Docs | Threat model (T1), CHANGELOG (breaking + migration), README, manifest spec. |

## Safety rails

Auto-advance only proceeds when every rail passes; otherwise the scope keeps its pin and reports (the run continues):

- **Fast-forward-only.** The new SHA must be a descendant of the current `trusted_sha`. Rewritten / force-pushed history is refused. This is the primary supply-chain rail now that human review is off the default path.
- **Revoke always wins.** The decision routes through `trust.Decide`; an active revoke refuses even under the auto posture. The posture is additionally **fail-closed without loaded trust state** — a caller that hasn't folded the trust store cannot auto-advance (structural, not caller-dependent).
- **Offline-safe.** If the remote is unreachable, sync falls back to the cached pin and warns; it never adopts an unverified head and never errors on a healthy cache.
- **Auditable.** Every advance re-pins `commit` + `trusted_sha` forward and records old→new to the structured log and the cache audit file.
- **Escapable.** `--frozen` and `canonical.auto: false` each restore today's deterministic, `update`-gated behavior. `local_dir` sources are untouched; `local_path` sources advance fast-forward-only.

## Breaking change & migration

On upgrade, a pinned workspace starts advancing on its next `sync`. To keep deterministic behavior:

```yaml
# .agent-sync.yaml — opt a workspace out permanently
canonical:
  auto: false
```

…or pass `--frozen` for a single run, and advance deliberately with `agent-sync update`. `docs/threat-model.md` (T1) documents the revised posture: fast-forward-only + the trust policy are the remaining rails, and commit-signature verification remains absent.

## Security review

Two adversarial review passes ran against the trust-touching code:

1. **Before activation** — found the revoke defense was *caller-contingent* (a caller could reach the auto path without loading revocation state). Fixed by making the posture fail-closed on unloaded state (`30fa697`).
2. **After activation** — found and fixed three issues before merge: a TOCTOU where the fast-forward baseline was read from a pre-lock manifest snapshot (now reloaded under the run lock); an empty-`trusted_sha` path that could materialize the floating-newest SHA unaudited (now ineligible for the auto path); and a trust-routing predicate that only agreed with source resolution because of the one-of manifest invariant (now aligned).

## Testing

- `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, full `go test -race ./...`, and `golangci-lint run` all green.
- New coverage includes: single-scope fast-forward advance with no prompt; `--frozen` and `canonical.auto:false` opt-outs producing pinned output; force-push refusal; a **revoked-anchor integration test** exercising the real `sync → Store.Fold → Decide` path; offline fallback to cached pin; fast-forward-baseline-vs-`trusted_sha` divergence; hierarchy mixed frozen/auto and refusal-continues; and pin-moved-but-emit-failed recovery.

Implementation of the units was delegated to Codex (`gpt-5.4`); the trust hardening, the three post-review fixes, and the docs were authored in Claude Code, which also orchestrated and reviewed each batch.

---

[![Compound Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_with-Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin)
![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Opus_4.8-D97757?logo=claude&logoColor=white)
__CE_PR_BODY_END__
gh pr create --title "feat(sync): inline auto-update — advance the canonical pin by default" --body-file "$BODY_FILE" 2>&1 | tail -5

15	preselection, `--target` now skips the wizard; plus sync user-scope offer
16	([Y/n] prompt via `hierarchySyncOptions.OfferUser` callback seam) and a
17	persistent skipped-user `notice` (text + JSON). Also fixed the duplicated
18	`<name>: started` stderr lines: adapterkit session banner (subprocess
19	proof-of-life) leaking from bundled in-process adapters — now `io.Discard`
20	in all five `bundled.go` files. Plan
21	`docs/plans/2026-07-06-001-feat-init-defaults-target-discovery-plan.md`
22	(R1–R18); master plan Unit 17 annotated (partial delivery; full branching
23	wizard + `docs/wizard/init-states.md` still open). Behavior changes: bare
24	`init --non-interactive` succeeds now; pin flags without source → named
25	error. Full gate green (36 pkgs -race, lint 0, GOOS=windows vet). User's
26	installed binary was v0.4.0 from /usr/local/bin — remind to reinstall after
27	release. 6 commits, TDD throughout.
28	
29	The agent-sync MVP is **done and shipped** — Unit 16 (CLI command tree + sync
30	orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
31	product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
32	hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
33	enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
34	validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.
35	
36	**v0.4.0 released 2026-07-02** (tag `v0.4.0`, stamp commit `7224ee9`): everything
37	below through PR #35 is now in a published release — pi adapter, scope-aware
38	`sync --user` (all 4 adapters), ADV-1 co-ownership + run-lock hardening, and
39	user-scope Cursor rule composition. Release workflow green, binary `--version`
40	verified. Next open items are the "Next candidates" list below, items 2–4
41	(pi `command`, `--break-lock`, Antigravity CLI adapter).
42	
43	**MERGED (2026-07-02): PR #38** (squash `b91321a` on main) — branch `feat/skill-descriptions-upstream-update`,
44	plan `docs/plans/2026-07-02-001-feat-skill-descriptions-upstream-update-plan.md`.
45	Two workstreams, 7 units (U1–U7), all committed, full `-race` gate + lint green.
46	**W1 skill descriptions:** `description` is now an authorable IR frontmatter field
47	(`internal/ir/kinds.go`,`types.go`); emitted `SKILL.md` is frontmatter-first
48	(`name`+`description` at byte 0) via a shared `internal/adapter/bundled/skillmeta`
49	helper (codex/pi byte-identical); managed headers interpolate real provenance
50	(`Source: <url>@<sha12>`, replacing the never-plumbed `{source-url}@{short-sha}`);
51	description-less skills emit-with-a-degraded-warning (capability-lie-gate safe).
52	**W2 update:** new `agent-sync update` (`internal/cli/cmd_update.go`) —
53	fast-forward-only re-pin (`IsAncestor(oldPin,newSHA)`) + gate (SHAs+ref+change
54	summary) + atomic `WriteResolvedSHA` + sync, all under one run lock via new
55	additive `engine.Options.RunLockHeld` (avoids the double-acquire deadlock).
56	Exit codes: 4 = needs `--accept-update`/`--accept-rewritten-history`, 6 = pin
57	moved but sync failed. Wire additions (`description`,`source_url`,`source_commit`
58	on node + InitializeParams) are additive/omitempty; SourceURL is always the
59	`cache.Canonicalize` form (no credential leak). Composed Cursor rules carry
60	per-node provenance (resolved open question). **Caveat:** ce-code-review was
61	spend-limit-degraded to an inline audit (subagents kept hitting the monthly
62	limit) — a full multi-persona pass before merge is prudent on this data-loss-
63	critical surface (engine lock seam + manifest mutation). See
64	[[spend-limit-degrades-review]]. Merged via --squash --admin (branch-protection
65	BLOCKED needs admin bypass, same as #36/#37; darwin/amd64 stall + CodeRabbit
66	queue were the only non-green checks — both non-blocking). Resolved 2 gemini
67	`t.Cleanup`-over-`defer` threads pre-merge (94a0481). **The Unreleased CHANGELOG
68	now holds three shipped-but-unreleased changes: #36 Antigravity retarget, #37
69	zero-emit hint, #38 skill descriptions + update — next `docs: stamp CHANGELOG
70	0.5.0` + tag `v0.5.0` release cuts them all** (note: canonical repos adopting
71	`description:` require ≥0.5.0). Remaining roadmap: pi `command`, `--break-lock`,
72	Antigravity CLI adapter.
73	
74	**To resume: read `docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md`**
75	— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
76	composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.
77	
78	**Latest state (2026-06-30):** All three bundled adapters are now scope-aware for
79	`sync --user`. PR #30 (merged, `378792f`) made the Claude adapter target
80	`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. PR #31 (**open**, branch
81	`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →
82	`~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; Cursor MCP → `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (no sidecar) with
83	rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for
84	them — confirmed against official docs 2026-06-30) and surfaced via
85	`internal/coverage` user-scope gap warnings. Plan:
86	`docs/plans/2026-06-30-001-fix-cursor-codex-scope-paths-plan.md`. See
87	[[claude-user-scope-gap]] (now resolved).
88	
89	**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
90	- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
91	  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
92	  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
93	- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
94	  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
95	  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
96	  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
97	  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
98	  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
99	  agents-md + skill.
100	
101	- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
102	  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
103	  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
104	  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
105	  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
106	  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
107	  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
108	  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.
109	
110	**Cursor/Pi/Codex parity effort (started 2026-07-04):** Audit found the
111	"unsupported" notes were STALE for Cursor (it shipped Agent Skills + flat
112	`.cursor/commands/`), accurate for Codex (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills are
113	by-design; no code — capability-honesty gate forbids forcing them).
114	- **PR #40 (OPEN, branch feat/cursor-skill):** Cursor `skill` → shared
115	  `.agents/skills/` tree (byte-identical SKILL.md, ADV-1 co-owned w/ codex/pi/
116	  antigravity), no engine change. Verified w/ cursor+codex co-ownership sync e2e.
117	- **PR #41 (OPEN, branch feat/file-leaf-output-mode):** the engine `file-leaf`
118	  OutputMode + Cursor command + Pi command. file-leaf = own individual FILES in a
119	  flat shared dir (never the dir/foreign files); reuses the single-file StagedWrite
120	  path (net-new = classification via fileLeafUnder → file is its own `effective`
121	  prefix; per-file drift/orphan ride ScanDrift/AdoptEntries which treat a file path
122	  as a 1-file walk → R10 fail-closed-on-collision for free). Cursor→.cursor/commands,
123	  Pi→.pi/prompts. Cursor now effective full parity. ce-code-review done (4 personas):
124	  fixed fileLeafUnder ./.. guard, mkdir-skip, runtime-gate continue-not-shortcircuit,
125	  + gate/multi-file/adopt tests. DEFERRED (documented in plan follow-up, no bundled
126	  trigger): content-aware orphan delete (cross-mode P0 of the existing content-blind
127	  orphan model — files carry "do not edit"), cross-adapter file-leaf co-ownership,
128	  declared-output overlap validation. Codex = no code (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills
129	  by design). Full gate green + real cursor+pi e2e (foreign files survive).
130	- **Deep plan for the rest:** `docs/plans/2026-07-04-001-feat-file-leaf-output-mode-plan.md`.
131	  Cursor `command` + Pi `command` are BLOCKED on a new engine **`file-leaf`
132	  OutputMode** (own individual files in a shared FLAT dir — `.cursor/commands/`,
133	  `.pi/prompts/` — co-resident w/ user files; Cursor CLI ignores command
134	  subdirs so flat-per-file is required). Key design (verified vs code): file-leaf
135	  REUSES the existing single-file StagedWrite path (`fileOutputs`/`fileType` in
136	  `internal/engine/target.go`); net-new logic is classification (a file-leaf
137	  child becomes its own `effective` prefix so `o.Path==pre` routes to the
138	  single-file branch) + per-file drift/orphan that NEVER walks the shared parent
139	  (foreign-file survival = the load-bearing safety prop). Data-loss-critical
140	  (AGENTS.md #6/#7) → TDD + mandatory ce-code-review. Plan is PR2 = engine
141	  file-leaf + pi command + cursor command; U-IDs U1-U8.
142	
143	**Next candidates (in order):**
144	1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
145	   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
146	   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
147	   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
148	   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
149	   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
150	   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
151	   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
152	   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
153	   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
154	   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
155	   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
156	   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
157	   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
158	   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
159	   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
160	   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
161	   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
162	   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
163	   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
164	   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
165	   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
166	   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
167	   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
168	   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
169	   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
170	   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
171	   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
172	   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
173	   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
174	   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
175	   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
176	   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
177	   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
178	   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
179	   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
180	   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
181	   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
182	   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
183	   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
184	2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
185	   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
186	3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
187	   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
188	4. ~~Antigravity CLI adapter~~ **LANDED 2026-07-03** — full-parity bundled
189	   adapter on branch `feat/antigravity-adapter` (commit `b06b2b8`, unreleased in
190	   CHANGELOG). Plan `docs/plans/2026-07-03-001-feat-antigravity-adapter-plan.md`,
191	   doc `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`. **Correction to the earlier prediction:**
192	   fresh 2026-07 research + the already-merged PR #36 IR decision both settle the
193	   overlay target on **GEMINI.md** (highest-precedence, tool-specific) — NO
194	   `.antigravity.md` file surfaced in current docs; that earlier note was stale.
195	   Real on-disk split (faithfully reproduced): rules/workflows under `.agent/`
196	   (SINGULAR, Windsurf lineage) → `.agent/rules/agent-sync/`,
197	   `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/`; skills/mcp under `.agents/` (PLURAL) →
198	   `.agents/skills/` (shared w/ codex/pi), `.agents/mcp_config.json`. agents-md →
199	   GEMINI.md managed section (NOT AGENTS.md — codex/pi own that). Scope-aware:
200	   agents-md/skill/mcp remap under `~/.gemini/` at user scope; rule+command have
201	   no Antigravity user-global home (coverage.nonNativeAtUser flags them inert).
202	   Verified via unit+integration+real `sync` e2e; go vet / test -race /
203	   golangci-lint all clean. **SHIPPED: PR #39 squash-merged to main (e05def1)
204	   via --squash --admin** (BLOCKED = review-required branch protection; darwin/amd64
205	   runner stall was the only pending check — infra, non-blocking; all other CI +
206	   CodeRabbit green). Resolved 1 CodeRabbit doc nit pre-merge (fcefac9: GEMINI.md
207	   managed-section id precision). **Released v0.6.0 2026-07-03** — CHANGELOG stamp
208	   8089b46, lightweight tag v0.6.0, goreleaser release.yml workflow triggered on
209	   tag. Remaining deferred adapters: windsurf, lmstudio, roo.
210	
211	See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].
212	
213	**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
214	dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
215	flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
216	(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so
217	`sync --target claude` hard-failed (codex/cursor were fixed earlier; claude was
218	not) — now emits inner body like the others; (2) **`validate` falsely reported
219	drift after a clean sync** for tool-owned files — `plan.go` marked every
220	ledgered path WouldUpdate unconditionally. Fix added `merge.DryMerge` (a
221	non-mutating dry-run of the shared merge dispatch that byte-compares merged-vs-
222	on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
223	plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
224	newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
225	`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
226	chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
227	passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup
228	(isJSONObject/markerOpenBytes across adapters), readExisting→readFileWithExists
229	rename, stale-slice cleanup for removed nodes in shared tool-owned files.
230	Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.
231	
232	**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
233	shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
234	rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
235	`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
236	`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
237	incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
238	break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
239	`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
240	Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
241	the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
242	newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).
243	
244	**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent
245	(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
246	lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.
247	
248	**Still out of scope (v1.0.0 gate):** pi adapter (11.5), Gemini + experimental
249	adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.
250	
251	The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`
252	+ `internal/sync/{staging,swap,recover}.go`, governed by AGENTS.md invariants #6
253	(two-rename atomic swap) and #7 (ledger authority). Run `ce-code-review` on any
254	change there — see [[spend-limit-degrades-review]].

- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
  agents-md + skill.

- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.

**Cursor/Pi/Codex parity effort (started 2026-07-04):** Audit found the
"unsupported" notes were STALE for Cursor (it shipped Agent Skills + flat
`.cursor/commands/`), accurate for Codex (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills are
by-design; no code — capability-honesty gate forbids forcing them).
- **PR #40 (OPEN, branch feat/cursor-skill):** Cursor `skill` → shared
  `.agents/skills/` tree (byte-identical SKILL.md, ADV-1 co-owned w/ codex/pi/
  antigravity), no engine change. Verified w/ cursor+codex co-ownership sync e2e.
- **PR #41 (OPEN, branch feat/file-leaf-output-mode):** the engine `file-leaf`
  OutputMode + Cursor command + Pi command. file-leaf = own individual FILES in a
  flat shared dir (never the dir/foreign files); reuses the single-file StagedWrite
  path (net-new = classification via fileLeafUnder → file is its own `effective`
  prefix; per-file drift/orphan ride ScanDrift/AdoptEntries which treat a file path
  as a 1-file walk → R10 fail-closed-on-collision for free). Cursor→.cursor/commands,
  Pi→.pi/prompts. Cursor now effective full parity. ce-code-review done (4 personas):
  fixed fileLeafUnder ./.. guard, mkdir-skip, runtime-gate continue-not-shortcircuit,
  + gate/multi-file/adopt tests. DEFERRED (documented in plan follow-up, no bundled
  trigger): content-aware orphan delete (cross-mode P0 of the existing content-blind
  orphan model — files carry "do not edit"), cross-adapter file-leaf co-ownership,
  declared-output overlap validation. Codex = no code (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills
  by design). Full gate green + real cursor+pi e2e (foreign files survive).
- **Deep plan for the rest:** `docs/plans/2026-07-04-001-feat-file-leaf-output-mode-plan.md`.
  Cursor `command` + Pi `command` are BLOCKED on a new engine **`file-leaf`
  OutputMode** (own individual files in a shared FLAT dir — `.cursor/commands/`,
  `.pi/prompts/` — co-resident w/ user files; Cursor CLI ignores command
  subdirs so flat-per-file is required). Key design (verified vs code): file-leaf
  REUSES the existing single-file StagedWrite path (`fileOutputs`/`fileType` in
  `internal/engine/target.go`); net-new logic is classification (a file-leaf
  child becomes its own `effective` prefix so `o.Path==pre` routes to the
  single-file branch) + per-file drift/orphan that NEVER walks the shared parent
  (foreign-file survival = the load-bearing safety prop). Data-loss-critical
  (AGENTS.md #6/#7) → TDD + mandatory ce-code-review. Plan is PR2 = engine
  file-leaf + pi command + cursor command; U-IDs U1-U8.

**Next candidates (in order):**
1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
4. ~~Antigravity CLI adapter~~ **LANDED 2026-07-03** — full-parity bundled
   adapter on branch `feat/antigravity-adapter` (commit `b06b2b8`, unreleased in
   CHANGELOG). Plan `docs/plans/2026-07-03-001-feat-antigravity-adapter-plan.md`,
   doc `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`. **Correction to the earlier prediction:**
   fresh 2026-07 research + the already-merged PR #36 IR decision both settle the
   overlay target on **GEMINI.md** (highest-precedence, tool-specific) — NO
   `.antigravity.md` file surfaced in current docs; that earlier note was stale.
   Real on-disk split (faithfully reproduced): rules/workflows under `.agent/`
   (SINGULAR, Windsurf lineage) → `.agent/rules/agent-sync/`,
   `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/`; skills/mcp under `.agents/` (PLURAL) →
   `.agents/skills/` (shared w/ codex/pi), `.agents/mcp_config.json`. agents-md →
   GEMINI.md managed section (NOT AGENTS.md — codex/pi own that). Scope-aware:
   agents-md/skill/mcp remap under `~/.gemini/` at user scope; rule+command have
   no Antigravity user-global home (coverage.nonNativeAtUser flags them inert).
   Verified via unit+integration+real `sync` e2e; go vet / test -race /
   golangci-lint all clean. **SHIPPED: PR #39 squash-merged to main (e05def1)
   via --squash --admin** (BLOCKED = review-required branch protection; darwin/amd64
   runner stall was the only pending check — infra, non-blocking; all other CI +
   CodeRabbit green). Resolved 1 CodeRabbit doc nit pre-merge (fcefac9: GEMINI.md
   managed-section id precision). **Released v0.6.0 2026-07-03** — CHANGELOG stamp
   8089b46, lightweight tag v0.6.0, goreleaser release.yml workflow triggered on
   tag. Remaining deferred adapters: windsurf, lmstudio, roo.

See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].

**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so
`sync --target claude` hard-failed (codex/cursor were fixed earlier; claude was
not) — now emits inner body like the others; (2) **`validate` falsely reported
drift after a clean sync** for tool-owned files — `plan.go` marked every
ledgered path WouldUpdate unconditionally. Fix added `merge.DryMerge` (a
non-mutating dry-run of the shared merge dispatch that byte-compares merged-vs-
on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup
(isJSONObject/markerOpenBytes across adapters), readExisting→readFileWithExists
rename, stale-slice cleanup for removed nodes in shared tool-owned files.
Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.

**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).

**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent
(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.

**Still out of scope (v1.0.0 gate):** pi adapter (11.5), Gemini + experimental
adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.

The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`
+ `internal/sync/{staging,swap,recover}.go`, governed by AGENTS.md invariants #6
(two-rename atomic swap) and #7 (ledger authority). Run `ce-code-review` on any
change there — see [[spend-limit-degrades-review]].

**PR #44 (OPEN, 2026-07-08, branch `feat/inline-auto-update`):** inline auto-update —
`agent-sync sync` advances the canonical pin to newest upstream **by default**
(semver-major posture flip). Plan `docs/plans/2026-07-08-001-feat-inline-auto-update-plan.md`,
brainstorm `docs/brainstorms/2026-07-08-inline-auto-update-requirements.md`. 8 units,
6 commits. Opt-outs: `--frozen` flag + `canonical.auto: false` (`*bool`, nil⇒on).
Rails: fast-forward-only; revoke always refuses; offline→cached-pin fallback;
old→new SHA to log + cache audit. Trust routed THROUGH `trust.Decide` via new
`PostureAllowNewSHAs`/`KindProceedAutoAdvance` — NOT bypassed; new `DecideInput.StateLoaded`
makes the posture fail-closed without a folded store (revoke stays load-bearing).
New `git.Input.ResolveOrFallback` materialize mode; shared `resolveAdvance`/`repinManifest`
extracted from `update` (`internal/cli/advance.go`); per-scope wiring in
`prepareScopeForSync`/`prepareAutoAdvanceScope` (`setup.go`), run-lock held across
re-pin+emit (RunLockHeld=true), hierarchy stays continue-and-report, pin-moved=exit 6.
Threat model T1 + CHANGELOG (breaking + migration) + README updated. **Implemented via
Codex delegation (`gpt-5.4`, ce-work-beta); trust hardening + 3 post-review fixes + docs
authored in Claude Code.** TWO adversarial review passes: pre-activation caught the
caller-contingent revoke defense (→ StateLoaded guard, commit 30fa697); post-activation
caught a TOCTOU ff-baseline (→ reload manifest under lock), an empty-`trusted_sha`
divergence (→ require trusted_sha for eligibility), and a routing-predicate coupling
(→ gate on !local_path) — all fixed in 8f1af84. Full `-race` gate + lint green.
local_path is ff-gated but NOT trust-gated (consistent w/ materializeLocal; revoke keyed
by URL). See [[spend-limit-degrades-review]], [[ci-darwin-amd64-runner-stall]].

**PR #42 (MERGED `e53317b`, branch `feat/init-defaults-user-sync-ux`):** zero-flag

content.text:
Done — **PR #44 is open: https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/pull/44**

Shipped the full inline auto-update feature across 6 commits on `feat/inline-auto-update`, with a narrative PR body covering the posture flip, safety rails, breaking-change migration, and both security-review passes. Also updated project memory (#42 merged, #44 now the active branch).

**Recap of the whole arc:**
- **Brainstorm → plan → execute** via Compound Engineering, with your three scope decisions (inline auto-update, new-default, all four rails) plus the trust-integration fork (route through the policy engine).
- **Execution** delegated to Codex (`gpt-5.4`) in four risk-ordered batches, each diff-reviewed and independently `-race`-verified — not trusted on self-report.
- **Two adversarial reviews** gated the trust-touching code: one caught a caller-contingent revoke defense (→ structural fail-closed guard I added), one caught a TOCTOU, an empty-anchor divergence, and a routing coupling (all three fixed and tested before commit).
- Final gate green: full `-race` suite + `golangci-lint`, 0 issues.

**Two things for your attention:**
1. **Title has no `!`/`BREAKING CHANGE:`** — I withheld the release-tooling major-bump trigger pending your call. It's semver-major and documented loudly in the body + CHANGELOG. Say the word if you want the `!` added so release-please bumps correctly on merge.
2. **CI** will now run, including the chronically-queued `test (darwin/amd64)` Intel runner — that's infra, not the code; don't fix-loop on it. The `darwin/arm64`, `linux`, and `windows` jobs are the real signal.

Want me to watch CI and handle any CodeRabbit feedback, or leave it here?
